caption: Two identical Spearmen-vs-Infantry standoffs, side by side, both taking comparable losses (24 -> 15 soldiers each by tick 720). Both regiments end up with about the same spread in file depth -- that's just the geometry of fitting fewer survivors into the same 7 files, either way. The real difference is WHICH file ends up shallowest: the left Spearmen (file_major_reform: true, the new default) settles on a real flank file by tick 600 and holds it through 720, because that's genuinely where more of its men fell. The right Spearmen (file_major_reform: false, the historical behavior) always settles on the dead-centre file instead, from tick 540 on, regardless of which files' soldiers actually died -- the grid keeps re-centring the shortfall onto the middle rather than tracking real losses.
